title: The Horizon of Questions

features.name: horizon-doubt
features.desc-on-touch: The line where certainty ends. You read your own question reflected back, distorted by distance.

features.name: foam-scatter
features.desc-on-touch: Language breaking into spray. Each bubble holds a word mid-dissolution.

features.name: interrogation-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: A mark that asks itself what marks are. The archive's deepest question about its own notation.

features.name: refraction-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Looking through it bends what you see. Your question returns as a different question.

features.name: edge-texture
features.desc-on-touch: The boundary where the archive's signal fades. You can hear the question in the static.

inscription: the horizon learned that asking is the only form of distance it understands

file.name: Horizon_Questions_Spectral_Decay.wav
file.note: An audio recording where each doubt shifts the frequency lower, until the question becomes inaudible.

exits.label: back out, where writing still has foam
upward to where doubt becomes language
further out, past the last visible question
